text: The surgeon isolates the stomach from surrounding adhesions, noting the organ's rich vascularization. Minor bleeding is observed as vessels are transected. Once isolated, the connection to the anterior abdominal wall is visible, and a stapler is used to seal and transect this portion of the stomach.

The surgeon leaves the staple line exposed, noting that leaks are uncommon, but prefers to imbricate it using a running monofilament suture. The remnant of the stomach connected to the anterior abdominal wall is dissected away and removed from the abdomen. The suture material is removed, and the single twelve millimeter port site is closed.
